$schema: internal — non-GLP-1 prescription drug weight-effect lookup data
verificationNotes: Every entry verified live against PubMed primary sources or FDA labels by a research subagent on 2026-04-08. Re-verify annually per the staleness audit recommendation. Magnitudes are intentionally honest — cited from the largest available trial, with effect sizes in absolute kg or % body weight where the trial reported them.

drugs.name: Metformin
drugs.magnitudeNote: −2.1 kg over 2.8 years in DPP; −5.8 kg over 6 months in non-diabetic obese (Seifarth 2013); −6.2% sustained at 15 years in metformin responders (DPPOS Apolzan 2019)
drugs.mechanism: Improves insulin sensitivity, reduces hepatic glucose production, modest appetite suppression
drugs.safetyNotes: Excellent long-term safety record (60+ years). Contraindicated in severe renal impairment. Hold around contrast imaging due to lactic acidosis risk.
drugs.interactionWithGlp1: No PK interaction. Standard of care to combine in T2D.

drugs.name: Topiramate
drugs.magnitudeNote: −6.3% body weight at 192-384 mg/day over 6 months (Bray 2003); −16.5% from baseline at 192 mg with low-calorie diet maintenance (Astrup 2004); off-label monotherapy for weight loss
drugs.mechanism: Appetite suppression via GABA-A modulation; altered taste perception; carbonic anhydrase inhibition
drugs.safetyNotes: Cognitive effects ('dopamax'); 10.7% kidney stones in long-term users (Maalouf 2010); metabolic acidosis (40-71%); cleft palate teratogenicity (RR 5.16 at >100 mg) — REMS in pregnancy.
drugs.interactionWithGlp1: No documented PK interaction. Off-label combination occasionally used by obesity-medicine specialists.

drugs.name: Phentermine + topiramate ER (Qsymia)
fdaApprovedFor: Chronic weight management (BMI ≥30, or ≥27 with comorbidity)
drugs.magnitudeNote: −10.2 kg / −9.8% at the 15/92 dose at 56 weeks (CONQUER); sustained −12.1% at 108 weeks (SEQUEL); 78.7% reduction in incident T2D (SEQUEL)
drugs.mechanism: Phentermine appetite suppression (sympathomimetic) + topiramate appetite suppression
drugs.safetyNotes: REMS-required for women of childbearing age (cleft palate risk from topiramate). Phentermine CV warnings still apply. Discontinuation rule: <5% loss at 12 weeks at 15/92 dose.
drugs.interactionWithGlp1: No PK interaction; off-label combination used cautiously by obesity-medicine specialists with CV monitoring.

drugs.name: Phentermine
drugs.magnitudeNote: Approximately −3.6 kg over 12 weeks at 15-37.5 mg/day in pooled trials. FDA approval is short-term only; long-term use is off-label.
drugs.mechanism: Releases norepinephrine and dopamine; appetite suppression
drugs.safetyNotes: Increases heart rate and blood pressure; contraindicated in cardiovascular disease, hyperthyroidism, glaucoma, MAOI use. DEA Schedule IV.
drugs.interactionWithGlp1: Off-label combination with GLP-1s is used clinically but has no RCT data. CV monitoring warranted.

drugs.name: Naltrexone + bupropion (Contrave)
fdaApprovedFor: Chronic weight management (BMI ≥30, or ≥27 with comorbidity)
drugs.magnitudeNote: −6.1% with NB 32/360 vs −1.3% placebo at 56 weeks (COR-I); 48% achieved ≥5% loss vs 16% placebo
drugs.mechanism: Bupropion (NDRI) reduces appetite via dopamine; naltrexone blocks opioid-mediated reward eating
drugs.safetyNotes: Bupropion seizure risk (~0.1%). Contraindicated in seizure disorder, eating disorders, abrupt sedative withdrawal. Common nausea (~30%).
drugs.interactionWithGlp1: No PK interaction documented. Combinations exist clinically but are off-label.

drugs.name: Bupropion (Wellbutrin)
drugs.magnitudeNote: −7.2% at 300 mg/day and −10.1% at 400 mg/day at 24 weeks (Anderson 2002); 7.5-8.6% sustained at 48 weeks. Off-label for weight; on-label for depression.
drugs.mechanism: NDRI; dopamine and norepinephrine effects on appetite and reward
drugs.safetyNotes: Seizure risk (dose-dependent). Contraindicated in seizure disorder and eating disorders. Common: insomnia, dry mouth.
drugs.interactionWithGlp1: No PK interaction. Effects on weight are additive with GLP-1s in the same direction.

drugs.name: Lisdexamfetamine (Vyvanse)
drugs.magnitudeNote: −4.9 kg as secondary outcome at 50-70 mg/day in BED trials over 11 weeks (McElroy 2015). Hudson 2017 maintenance trial: 3.7% relapse on Vyvanse vs 32.1% placebo over 6 months.
drugs.mechanism: Catecholamine release; appetite suppression
drugs.safetyNotes: DEA Schedule II. Boxed warning for sudden death, MI, stroke. Psychiatric: psychosis, mania risk. Avoid in CV disease.
drugs.interactionWithGlp1: No PK interaction documented. Theoretical additive cardiovascular and appetite-suppression concerns. Combination not RCT-tested.

drugs.name: Mixed amphetamine salts (Adderall)
drugs.magnitudeNote: No RCT specifically for weight loss in adults. Pediatric ADHD literature documents 1-2 kg weight effect from chronic stimulant treatment. Off-label use for weight loss is not evidence-based.
drugs.vsGlp1Magnitude: Not applicable — not approved for weight loss
drugs.mechanism: Catecholamine release; appetite suppression
drugs.safetyNotes: DEA Schedule II. Same CV and psychiatric warnings as Vyvanse. Currently in shortage as of April 2026.
drugs.interactionWithGlp1: Off-label and not evidence-based for weight loss.

drugs.name: Dulaglutide (Trulicity)
drugs.magnitudeNote: −3.1 kg at 1.5 mg, −4.0 kg at 3.0 mg, −4.7 kg at 4.5 mg in T2D patients (AWARD-11). Smallest weight effect of the modern GLP-1s.
drugs.mechanism: GLP-1 receptor agonist (same class as semaglutide)
drugs.safetyNotes: Same boxed warning for thyroid C-cell tumors as other GLP-1s. GI side effects.
drugs.interactionWithGlp1: Don't combine — same drug class.

drugs.name: Empagliflozin (Jardiance)
drugs.magnitudeNote: Approximately 2-3 kg from urinary glucose excretion (~70 g/day → ~280 kcal/day deficit). Major value is the cardiovascular and kidney outcome benefits, not weight.
drugs.mechanism: Blocks SGLT2 in proximal renal tubule → glucose excretion in urine
drugs.safetyNotes: Genital mycotic infections (~6% vs 2% placebo). Rare DKA, Fournier's gangrene. Volume depletion in elderly.
drugs.interactionWithGlp1: Combination is now first-line in T2D + obesity + cardio-renal risk per ADA 2025.

drugs.name: Dapagliflozin (Farxiga)
drugs.magnitudeNote: ~2-3 kg via urinary glucose excretion. Major indications are heart failure (DAPA-HF, DELIVER) and CKD (DAPA-CKD), not weight.
drugs.mechanism: Blocks SGLT2 in proximal renal tubule
drugs.safetyNotes: Same class effects as Jardiance.
drugs.interactionWithGlp1: Combination first-line in T2D + obesity + cardio-renal risk per ADA 2025.

drugs.name: Spironolactone
drugs.magnitudeNote: No meaningful weight effect in general populations. Used off-label for PCOS hyperandrogenism (acne, hirsutism), not weight loss. Patients sometimes notice short-term water weight changes from diuresis.
drugs.mechanism: Aldosterone antagonist; potassium-sparing diuresis; anti-androgen
drugs.safetyNotes: Hyperkalemia risk; gynecomastia in men; teratogenic.
drugs.interactionWithGlp1: No PK interaction. Sometimes co-prescribed in PCOS patients on a GLP-1.

drugs.name: HRT (estrogen ± progestogen)
drugs.magnitudeNote: No significant effect on body weight per Norman 2000 Cochrane review (28 RCTs, n=28,559). HRT does NOT prevent menopausal weight gain.
drugs.mechanism: Estrogen receptor activation
drugs.safetyNotes: WHI cardiovascular and breast cancer signals; NAMS 2022 favors HRT for women under 60 / within 10 years of menopause.
drugs.interactionWithGlp1: Prefer transdermal HRT over oral when patient is on a GLP-1 (especially Foundayo or tirzepatide) due to gastric emptying interaction with oral hormonal contraceptives.

drugs.name: Sertraline (Zoloft)
drugs.magnitudeNote: Small long-term weight gain in cohort data (Gafoor 2018 BMJ: 21% higher rate of ≥5% gain across all antidepressants combined). Sertraline is among the more weight-neutral SSRIs.
drugs.mechanism: Serotonin reuptake inhibition
drugs.safetyNotes: GI side effects, sexual dysfunction.
drugs.interactionWithGlp1: No PK interaction. Watch for additive nausea early in GLP-1 titration; staggered initiation is the workaround.

drugs.name: Escitalopram (Lexapro)
drugs.magnitudeNote: Modest long-term weight gain (Maina 2004 in OCD: greater gain than sertraline / fluoxetine). Among the more weight-positive SSRIs.
drugs.mechanism: Selective serotonin reuptake inhibition
drugs.safetyNotes: GI, sexual dysfunction.
drugs.interactionWithGlp1: No PK interaction.

drugs.name: Paroxetine (Paxil)
drugs.magnitudeNote: The most weight-positive SSRI. Significantly more ≥7% weight gain than fluoxetine or sertraline (Fava 2000). Mechanism likely anticholinergic + antihistamine activity.
drugs.mechanism: Serotonin reuptake inhibition + anticholinergic + antihistamine
drugs.safetyNotes: GI, sexual dysfunction, withdrawal syndrome on cessation.
drugs.interactionWithGlp1: No PK interaction. Consider switching to a weight-neutral SSRI (sertraline) or weight-loss antidepressant (bupropion) if weight is a major concern.

drugs.name: Mirtazapine (Remeron)
drugs.magnitudeNote: 1.4-3.6 kg gain in first 6-8 weeks across multiple trials, plateauing around 6-8 months. Mechanism: appetite stimulation via histamine H1 antagonism. Most weight-positive antidepressant in common use.
drugs.mechanism: H1 antihistamine activity drives appetite stimulation; serotonergic + adrenergic effects
drugs.safetyNotes: Sedation; weight gain; rare agranulocytosis.
drugs.interactionWithGlp1: No PK interaction. Avoid as first-line in patients trying to lose weight; bupropion or vortioxetine are better choices.

drugs.name: Vortioxetine (Trintellix)
drugs.magnitudeNote: Weight-neutral per FDA label and Mahableshwarkar 2015 Phase 3 trial. Cognitive benefits in addition to mood effects.
drugs.mechanism: Multimodal serotonergic activity
drugs.safetyNotes: GI side effects, sexual dysfunction (less than other SSRIs).
drugs.interactionWithGlp1: No PK interaction.

drugs.name: Rosiglitazone (Avandia)
drugs.magnitudeNote: +4.8 kg vs +1.6 kg glyburide vs −2.8 kg metformin at 4 years (ADOPT trial Kahn 2006). Worst T2D oral drug for weight.
drugs.mechanism: PPAR-γ activation drives adipogenesis and fluid retention
drugs.safetyNotes: Heart failure boxed warning; bone fracture risk in women. Use is now uncommon.
drugs.interactionWithGlp1: Avoid combining; metabolic profile cancels GLP-1 weight benefit.

drugs.name: Glyburide
drugs.magnitudeNote: +1.6 kg at 4 years (ADOPT trial). Sulfonylureas as a class cause modest weight gain via insulin release.
drugs.mechanism: Stimulates pancreatic insulin release
drugs.safetyNotes: Hypoglycemia risk (more than other oral drugs). Largely supplanted by newer classes.
drugs.interactionWithGlp1: Increases hypoglycemia risk when combined; dose reduction often needed.
